codeReview.summary: All 31 files updated correctly. Zero remaining inbound.new references in scoped directories (lib/, app/api/, app/actions/, app/vercel-oss-program/, emails/, components/email-banner.tsx). Sender addresses consistently use NOTIFICATION_DOMAIN, URLs use APP_URL/DOCS_URL/API_BASE_URL from lib/config/app-url.ts. Import paths use @/ alias throughout. Two non-blocking issues noted.

issues.description: Import statement `import { toast } from 'sonner'` appears after non-import code (const APP_HOST/supportEmail declarations at lines 12-19). ES module imports are hoisted so this works, but violates conventional import ordering. The domain extraction logic also duplicates extractDomain() from lib/config/app-url.ts, though this duplication is justified because this is a 'use client' component and lib/config/app-url.ts mixes server-only env vars (INTERNAL_BASE_URL, SUPPORT_EMAIL) making it unsafe to import from client components.

Fallback `${SUPPORT_EMAIL || "support"}` renders as just the word 'support' when SUPPORT_EMAIL is not set (it defaults to empty string in app-url.ts). The original text was 'Contact support@inbound.new' — the new version loses the email address in the fallback case. Renders as 'Contact support to discuss resuming service.' which is acceptable prose but less actionable than the original.

sharedStateObservations.observation: lib/config/app-url.ts mixes server-only env vars (INTERNAL_BASE_URL, SUPPORT_EMAIL, LEGAL_EMAIL) with client-accessible env vars (NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL). This makes the module unsafe to import from 'use client' components, forcing the worker to duplicate domain extraction logic in app/vercel-oss-program/page.tsx. AGENTS.md documents the module's exports but doesn't note this client/server import constraint.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/vercel-oss-program/page.tsx lines 12-19 duplicate extractDomain() logic from lib/config/app-url.ts because the component is 'use client' and can't safely import the server-mixed module.

sharedStateObservations.observation: SUPPORT_EMAIL defaults to empty string in lib/config/app-url.ts, which means template fallbacks like `${SUPPORT_EMAIL || 'support'}` resolve to just the word 'support' rather than a full email address. If the intent is self-hosting where SUPPORT_EMAIL must be configured, this is fine. If a more useful default is expected, the config module should provide one (e.g., `support@${NOTIFICATION_DOMAIN}`).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: lib/config/app-url.ts line 9: `export const SUPPORT_EMAIL = process.env.SUPPORT_EMAIL || "";` — emails/reputation-alert.tsx line 101 uses `${SUPPORT_EMAIL || "support"}` as fallback text.

summary: PASS. The feature comprehensively replaces all hardcoded inbound.new references across the 31 scoped files. All sender addresses use NOTIFICATION_DOMAIN, all URLs use config-driven values from lib/config/app-url.ts. Verification grep confirms zero remaining matches in scope. Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) import ordering in vercel-oss-program/page.tsx, and (2) SUPPORT_EMAIL fallback renders as plain text 'support' rather than an email address. Both are minor and don't affect functionality. The worker followed the refactor-worker skill procedure correctly.
